title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport represents UK aviation during visit to China as trade talks progress

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport attended a diplomatic reception hosted by the British Embassy in Shanghai as UK-China trade discussions continued. Prestwick said it was the only aviation business invited by Ambassador Peter Wilson and Consul-General Matt Burney, joining Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, Business Secretary Peter Kyle and other business leaders from the UK and China.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The airport said it became the UK base for Air China Cargo and China Southern Airlines in summer 2025. Building on routes to Guangzhou and Chengdu, Prestwick now operates 12 scheduled cargo services direct to China each week, a network it said has created more than 250 direct jobs and could facilitate GBP 250 million of cross-border trade in 2026.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Colin Dai said the visit let Prestwick share practical insight on how aviation and logistics infrastructure can support sustainable trade growth, including progress on tariffs. The airport also said Royal Mail and EVRi have established e-commerce operations there, and that a GBP 1 million investment in cool chain facilities supported a Scotland to China seafood export service and workforce growth of more than 250.
